title: Stale-docs rules (starter list, deliberately narrow, adjustable)

plain_language.what_this_is: A small, explicit starter list of 'if this kind of code changed, this doc should have been updated too.' Kept narrow on purpose to avoid false alarms; tighten and grow it from real misses rather than designing the whole policy up front.
plain_language.how_it_reads: Each rule has when_changed (code globs) and expect_updated (docs). If any file matching when_changed changed on the branch but the expected doc(s) did NOT, the stale-docs signal flags it. If no rule matches the changes, the signal stays QUIET -- it never guesses.
plain_language.adjustable: Add rules as you find real misses; remove any that cry wolf. This is a starter, not a fixed policy.

rules.why: A user-facing app change is a feature; the roadmap should reflect it.
A detector script is a feature; the roadmap should reflect it.
Verification wiring / fallback behavior is load-bearing; both the brief and the roadmap should reflect a change to it.

not_yet_automated: Standing product-direction / architecture changes should be reflected in docs/DECISIONS.md or PROJECT.md, but 'a direction change' has no clean code-file trigger, so it is left to human/verifier judgment for now rather than faked into a false alarm.
